question_text: A book is resting on a table and not moving. Which statement best explains why the book stays stationary?

options.label: A
options.text: The forces acting on the book are balanced, so there is no resultant force

options.label: B
options.text: There are no forces acting on the book at all

options.label: C
options.text: Gravity is stronger than the normal force from the table

options.label: D
options.text: The book has no mass so it cannot move

explanation: According to Newton's first law, an object stays at rest if the forces on it are balanced. The weight of the book (downward) is balanced by the normal force from the table (upward), so the resultant force is zero and the book doesn't move.
